mechanism_narrative: A low unemployment rate below 4% compresses the labor pool and forces upward wage pressure, squeezing corporate profit margins and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as the cost shock is not offset by pricing power.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for UNRATE level against 20-day forward returns is consistently positive or insignificant, or S&P 500 positive returns occur in more than 60% of periods when UNRATE is below 4%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the unemployment rate above 4.0% signals the onset or deepening of a recessionary contraction in aggregate demand, which erodes corporate earnings and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:unemployment level above 4% historically precedes recessions
failure_condition: After UNRATE crosses above 4.0%, average 60-day S&P 500 return is positive or not significantly different from pre-trigger periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production (INDPRO) while payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth remains strong reveals a sectoral imbalance where manufacturing weakness is not yet reflected in labor numbers, presaging a broader economic slowdown that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines month-over-month for 2 consecutive months and PAYEMS monthly change is below 100,000
evidence_refs: fact:divergence between manufacturing and services often precedes recessions
failure_condition: The joint trigger fires, but subsequent 20-day S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 50% of cases or the signal's Rank IC is negative over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle (elevated and/or rising FEDFUNDS), persistent high inflation (CPIAUCSL) forces the central bank to maintain or accelerate rate hikes, increasing discount rates and depressing equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 3.0% and not cutting, high inflation (CPIAUCSL year-over-year > 3%) is followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of months, or the signal's IC flips sign.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in real retail sales (RSAFS) signals strong consumer spending and rising corporate revenues, leading to upgraded earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive month-over-month RSAFS changes do not produce a statistically significant positive average 20-day S&P 500 return (p > 0.05) in an out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in the federal funds rate below 4.0% signals the end of a tightening cycle and the beginning of monetary accommodation, which lowers discount rates and boosts equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: end of tightening cycle, pivot to easing
failure_condition: After FEDFUNDS drops below 4.0%, the average 20-day S&P 500 return is negative or not significantly positive (p > 0.05), or the signal produces negative returns in more than half of occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: High interest rates (FEDFUNDS above 4.5%) combined with declining housing starts (HOUST month-over-month declines for two months) signal that restrictive monetary policy is curbing housing demand, which propagates to broader economic weakness and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and HOUST decreases m/m for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: The joint trigger fires, but next 60-day S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 50% of cases or the absolute return is smaller than 1.5 standard deviations of the unconditional distribution.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate is on a rising trend (3-month moving average increasing), a decline in industrial production amplifies the negative growth signal by confirming that sectors beyond housing are weakening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days as earnings downgrades accelerate.
failure_condition: During periods when UNRATE 3-month MA is rising, the interaction between INDPRO declines and S&P 500 60-day returns is not significantly negative (p > 0.05), or the signal fails in three consecutive NBER recession episodes.
